title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 42 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Indonesia's official national celebration marking the start of the calendar year with public holidays and festivities across all regions.

festivals.name: Isra and Mi'raj
festivals.description: Islamic holiday commemorating Prophet Muhammad's night journey and ascension to heaven, observed with prayers and spiritual gatherings.

festivals.name: Chinese New Year
festivals.description: Celebrated by Indonesia's Chinese communities with vibrant parades, dragon dances, and family gatherings, particularly lively in Jakarta and Medan.

festivals.name: Nyepi (Balinese New Year)
festivals.description: The Balinese Hindu New Year marked by the Day of Silence where the entire island shuts down for introspection and spiritual cleansing, preceded by exuberant Ogoh-Ogoh parade.

festivals.name: Ramadan
festivals.description: The Islamic holy month of fasting observed throughout Indonesia with special prayers, family gatherings, and vibrant night markets in every city.

festivals.name: Eid al-Fitr (Lebaran)
festivals.description: Indonesia's biggest celebration marking the end of Ramadan with massive holiday exodus, family reunions, and feasting that dominates national life.

festivals.name: Makepung Jembrana Buffalo Race
festivals.description: Traditional water buffalo racing in Bali's Jembrana Regency where teams compete with decorated buffalo pulling wooden carts across rice paddies.

festivals.name: Vesakha Buddha Day
festivals.description: National holiday honoring Buddha's birth, enlightenment, and death with Buddhist ceremonies, temple pilgrimages, and candlelit processions.

festivals.name: Flores Komodo Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the unique Komodo dragon and Flores culture with ceremonies, traditional dances, and showcases of local craftsmanship in this remote island region.

festivals.name: Eid al-Adha (Lebaran Haji)
festivals.description: Islamic festival of sacrifice commemorating Abraham's willingness to sacrifice his son, marked by animal sacrifices and distribution of meat to the poor.

festivals.name: Tumpak Landhep (Balinese Harvest Festival)
festivals.description: Ancient Balinese harvest thanksgiving festival featuring temple ceremonies, offerings, and traditional rituals celebrating the agricultural cycle.

festivals.name: Islamic New Year
festivals.description: The Islamic calendar's new year observed with special prayers and religious gatherings across Muslim-majority Indonesia.

festivals.name: Mawlid Nabi Muhammad
festivals.description: Birthday of Prophet Muhammad celebrated nationwide with Quranic recitations, sermon gatherings, and special prayers in mosques.

festivals.name: Garuda Wisnu Kencana Festival
festivals.description: Cultural festival at the iconic GWK statue featuring traditional dance performances, kite flying, and Balinese handicraft exhibitions.

festivals.name: Sade Village Festival
festivals.description: Cultural celebration in the traditional Sasak village showcasing unique indigenous traditions, weaving crafts, and horse racing.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: Indonesia's most important national holiday commemorating independence from Dutch colonial rule with flag ceremonies, parades, and competitions nationwide.

festivals.name: Jakarta Fashion Week
festivals.description: Major fashion event showcasing Indonesian and international designers with runway shows and exhibitions in Southeast Asia's fashion capital.

festivals.name: Bali Arts Festival
festivals.description: Month-long celebration of Balinese arts featuring traditional dance, music, theater, painting, and sculpture performances at venues across Bali.

festivals.name: Rajah Fishing Festival
festivals.description: Traditional fishing competition in Balikpapan celebrating maritime heritage with boat races and traditional fishing demonstrations.

festivals.name: Manado Fiesta
festivals.description: Manado's main cultural festival featuring street parades, traditional music and dance performances, and exhibitions celebrating Minahasan culture.

festivals.name: Bathik Day
festivals.description: National celebration of Indonesia's UNESCO-recognized batik tradition with exhibitions, fashion shows, and workshops showcasing this iconic textile art.

festivals.name: Dayak Harvest Festival (Gawai Dayak)
festivals.description: Traditional harvest celebration of Dayak people featuring ritual ceremonies, traditional dance, and communal feasting in longhouses.
festivals.location: West Kalimantan and Sarawak border regions

festivals.name: Pushaka Festival
festivals.description: Palembang's cultural festival celebrating the city's heritage with traditional sports, music performances, and historical exhibitions.

festivals.name: Hang Nadim Festival
festivals.description: Batam's maritime festival honoring the legendary Hang Nadim with boat races, sea festivals, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Mentawai Island Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of Mentawai indigenous culture featuring traditional tattoo exhibitions, tribal ceremonies, and surfing events on the island's world-famous waves.

festivals.name: Jember Fashion Carnival
festivals.description: Flamboyant street parade featuring elaborate costumes and creative float designs celebrating fashion, culture, and creativity in East Java.

festivals.name: Karimun Jawa Carnival
festivals.description: Island festival showcasing marine and maritime culture with boat races, traditional ceremonies, and seafood celebrations.

festivals.name: Borobudur Sunrise Festival
festivals.description: Spiritual celebration at the ancient Buddhist temple featuring meditation gatherings, traditional performances, and cultural activities at sunrise.

festivals.name: Ramayana Ballet at Prambanan
festivals.description: Open-air theatrical performance of the Hindu epic Ramayana against the backdrop of the illuminated 10th-century Prambanan temple.

festivals.name: Haul Syech Yusuf
festivals.description: Makassar's commemorative festival honoring Syech Yusuf with religious gatherings, Quranic recitations, and traditional ceremonies.

festivals.name: Kasepuhan Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of Sundanese indigenous culture and traditional leadership systems with ceremonies, traditional dress, and communal rituals.

festivals.name: Puncak Pass Festival
festivals.description: Mountain cultural festival featuring traditional Sundanese arts, crafts, and cuisine in the cool highlands near Jakarta.

festivals.name: Wonderful Indonesia Festival
festivals.description: National festival celebrating Indonesia's diverse cultures, cuisines, and traditions with performances and exhibitions from across the archipelago.

festivals.name: Labuan Bajo Jazz Festival
festivals.description: International jazz festival in the scenic port town near Komodo National Park featuring performances by regional and international jazz musicians.

festivals.name: Yogyakarta Craft Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of traditional Javanese batik, woodcarving, and silversmithing crafts with artisan exhibitions, workshops, and sales.

festivals.name: Banyuwangi Ethno Carnival
festivals.description: East Java's major cultural parade featuring elaborate floats, traditional costumes, and performances celebrating the region's ethnic diversity.

festivals.name: Halmahera Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of Maluku spice islands heritage with traditional ceremonies, spice exhibitions, and demonstrations of historic maritime trade.

festivals.name: Toraja Funeral Festival (Ma'nene)
festivals.description: Unique ancient ritual where deceased ancestors are exhumed, dressed, and paraded through villages in colorful ceremonies celebrating the cycle of life.

festivals.name: Banjarmasin Floating Market Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of Banjarmasin's iconic floating markets with traditional boat races, waterfront performances, and showcases of local produce.

festivals.name: Lampung Way Kambas Festival
festivals.description: Wildlife festival celebrating endangered Sumatran elephants with environmental awareness activities, animal demonstrations, and conservation exhibits.

festivals.name: Aceh Culture Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of Acehnese heritage featuring traditional war dances, poetry recitations, and exhibitions of Islamic arts and crafts.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrated nationwide with religious services, family gatherings, and festive decorations in churches and Christian communities.
